topics.title: Cancelled Guests and Joe Rogan
topics.summary: The studio discusses the frustration of last-minute cancellations by guests like John Cusack and Mark McGrath, followed by a discussion regarding Joe Rogan's perceived grudge over past comments about his attitude toward women.

topics.title: Chess Quiz and Workplace Conflict
topics.summary: The studio features a caller who fails a basic chess quiz before the conversation shifts to a heated recording of an office manager confronting a former intern over unpaid bar tabs, followed by a discussion regarding personal relationships and infidelity.

topics.title: Marital Conflict and Emotional Distance
topics.summary: The studio discusses the complexities of a man's marriage, focusing on his emotional distance, his wife's lack of accountability regarding an affair, and their efforts to reconcile through therapy.

topics.title: Marital Infidelity and Relationship Dynamics
topics.summary: The studio explores the complexities of marital infidelity, personal insecurities regarding physical attributes, and the dynamics of maintaining a marriage after an emotional affair.

topics.title: Dating Dynamics and Health Rituals
topics.summary: The studio explores the complexities of non-committal dating, personal anecdotes regarding past radio careers, and the details of a rigorous detox diet.

topics.title: Satellite Radio and Industry Awards
topics.summary: The studio discusses the complexities of satellite radio regulations, the legitimacy of Telly Awards won by the production team, and personal career milestones including a Canadian Walk of Fame induction for producer Ivan Reitman.

topics.title: Celebrity Lists and Upcoming Dates
topics.summary: The studio discusses a list of notable actors and athletes, followed by a segment involving a caller named Eric who debates whether to have a camera crew accompany him on an upcoming date.

topics.title: Relationship Dynamics and Parenting Decisions
topics.summary: The studio explores the complexities of navigating personal boundaries, the impact of external advice on marital intimacy, and the specific challenges of reconciling differing desires regarding having children.

topics.title: Relationship Dynamics and Parenting Conflicts
topics.summary: The show features a discussion regarding the complexities of navigating differing views on children within a relationship, followed by a segment featuring a young spelling bee champion.

topics.title: Trivia Game and Prank Calls
topics.summary: The studio discusses a spelling competition involving the word "scombered" and reacts to various prank calls before transitioning into a trivia game regarding general knowledge questions about American history.

topics.title: Trivia Contest and Gossip Game
topics.summary: The studio hosts a trivia segment featuring questions about history, geography, and math before transitioning to a gossip game with Mike Walker.

topics.title: Celebrity Rumors and Show Feedback
topics.summary: The studio reviews various celebrity news stories, including a dispute over a prop's cost, a potential television partnership between Rosie O'Donnell and Don Imus, and listener feedback regarding recent segments.

topics.title: Hayden Interview and Playboy Photos
topics.summary: The show features a phone interview with Hayden, who discusses her appearance in Playboy, her relationship with a staff member named Skippy, and the production of Howard 100 News.

topics.title: Dan Rather and Katie Couric News Controversy
topics.summary: The studio discusses Dan Rather's criticism of Katie Couric as a news anchor, the distinction between journalism and entertainment, and the risks of show business contracts.

topics.title: Kurt Waldheim Death and Fertility Trends
topics.summary: The show covers the death of Austrian former president Kurt Waldheim, the rise in IVF usage among women over 40, a new song about Barack Obama, and reports on a prison escapee and potential prostitution legalization in Mexico City.

topics.title: Sports and News Updates
topics.summary: The show covers the potential suspension of baseball player Jason Giambi, reports on civil unrest in the Middle East, updates on Iraq's security situation, news regarding space station malfunctions, and a report on Ruth Graham's health.

transcript.text: "Yes, good morning for you. To you. Not for you. Alright, a good morning for you. Why not? It's Thursday. Such a weird day yesterday, you know."
"We were supposed to have a bunch of guests today, and believe me, I'm not bitching that they're not coming in. I could care a lot."
"Somebody canceled?" "Well, it was hard for Gary. Gary had been calling me all week. John Cusack, Mark McGrath, and Jimmy Fallon were all booked for today."
"That's a big show." "Yeah, that's like the Conan show."
"Yeah, I think it was a whole big deal. With our show, it's kind of hard to get three guests in, so we asked Jimmy if he could move over to next week because he's around.
And he said, 'Yeah, sure.' You know, he's a good guy, Jimmy Fallon. And then Cusack's people called Gary, and the McGrath's people called Gary, and both canceled."
"Wow." "So it was like, you know, and Gary knows I don't care, but the only reason I care is because they drive Gary crazy."
"Well, he works hard to make these bookings and, you know, accommodate people, and then you get all ready for them, you clear the schedule, and boom, they're gone."
"You know who had the toughest day is Will, because Will does heavy duty research."
"Yeah, we research these guys, because, you know, you've got to make sure it's fresh."
"And we didn't think Cusack was coming on until yesterday, and in all fairness, I should say, it was never confirmed, but it was 99 percent confirmed, and then it sort of went away.
But Will just was here until 7 o'clock last night doing research on John Cusack. You know, he does like 10 pages."
"That's late. That's late to be." "That's a long day."
"Because he's here now." "Yeah, Will gets here when we do, yeah."
"So what happened? Like, they call you up and go, 'Hey, we just can't come?'"
"You know, with the Cusack people, it's a weird thing. The movie company, of course, wants it to happen because they've got the movie, so their intentions are just, 'We want to get the guy on.'"
"I didn't see any reviews of the movie today. What's the name of the movie?"
"1408. Roper and Ebert gave it two thumbs up." "Oh, okay." "And I saw most of it, and I thought it was really good. It reminded me a lot. It feels a lot like The Shining. It's a Stephen King book."
"We forget this isn't Friday for us, too. You know, it's Thursday still, so it's Friday." "Oh, that's what it is."
"Tomorrow." "You were absolutely right. I did. I looked for the movie reviews. I was like, 'Yeah, look.'"
"I make that same mistake." "All the time. I do, too."

"Although there was a movie review of Fantastic Four today, one of the newspapers made bomb that I was kind of bummed. I was hoping it was going to be good. Now I'm almost like, 'I don't even know if I want to see it.'"
"It's almost like if Jessica Alba's in a movie. Expect it to be bad."
"Yeah, why is that?" "Expect it to be hot."
"She's not even hot. The first Fantastic Four, she's not even hot."
"I never saw her in that. She's too close."
"I mean, that's the point. You're a big fan of hers, and you don't go to see her movie."
"I think I have Honey on DVD. I have screening parties for Honey." "But she's not sexual. She's like this girl woman."
"Mannequin? She's like a mannequin."
"Pick up Honey. Pick up Honey. Tell me if that's not a mannequin you want to fuck."
"What is she doing, Honey? Is she a dancer?"
"You know what? She's a dancer, like a hip-hop dancer, whatever."
"I mean, she's going to save people with her dance."
"That type of thing." "Asshole."
"She's trying to save a neighborhood. Look, I'm not saying I'm into the dialogue, but the outfits they put her in are just extraordinary. They show off her abs and her abs."
"She has camel tushes, camel toe in Fantastic Four."
"Yeah, well, with that outfit, I'm sure she would."
"Arty, can you really watch that movie for any length of time now?"
"Well, I don't know."
"Honestly, I don't have it on DVD, but when it's been on cable, I honestly, with the mute on, have watched almost the whole fucking thing."
"With like, you know, the Allman Brothers eat a peach on or something."
"So, guys, when you fix the microphone on Arty, the whole balance is still off. Now, I'm struggling."
"I can't say. I can't."
"You're louder than everyone."
"Yeah."
"Yeah, wow."
"I mean, it's weird. When I opened my mouth, I was like, 'Boom!'"
"It's giving me a headache. It's so loud. Guys, you got to put it back somewhat. I don't know what you're doing."
"I shouldn't have said anything."
"I don't want to."
"When I say something, man, then it gets crazy. I need me to be loud so I don't strain. I'll go over this off the air."
"We'll get it worked out, don't worry."
"Every break Scott came in here today, he was looking at my mic, grabbing it, moving it."
"Yeah, I don't want to."
"Here's what we'll do."
"I'm struggling right now. I don't want this to drag on."
"Here's what we'll do. They set the settings back and I'll work on it when I have a free day."
"It's a different story's microphone yesterday. It's a different microphone, so I'll switch it back."
"Just switch it back and let's get back from business because I'm going to fucking tell someone."
"I told you I'm angry."
"It's also, again, I don't break your balls, Scott, and make your day worse."
"But he also told me to keep it a certain angle and that takes away the whole fun of these mics."
"Like if something's happening over there, he wants me to keep it this way."
"Look at Scott. Is there fun in Scott?"
"Look, he's an engineer. That's stuff I don't get and it's probably a pain in the ass."
"I tell you what, can they change the mics back now and I'll take a break?"
"Sure."
"I can't worry about mic angles when I'm making mic comedy."
"I don't want you to take a break to find out that..."
"It's distracting the shit out of me."
"If I move back?"
"No, no, no, no. This is not your problem. This is a problem."
"Now you have to go into another room, Artie."
"Artie, you'll have to go home today."
"Hey, I got to get the ISDN."
"There you go."
"I'm all in, brother."
"Anyway, I was saying something, but now I'm totally distracted."
"What was I talking about?"
"Jessica Alba."
"Oh, the canceling of the guests."
"John Kuzek, that's right."
"What, tell me about John Kuzek."
"So he was never booked on the show, but they were, you know, they kept calling asking about availability."
"I'm moving stuff, I said I have to move stuff around. I move stuff around to make it ready for him."
"John's getting in a plane on noon, we'll call you back."
"They call me to ask me more questions. How long will he be there?"
"I said 45 minutes. Could it be 30 minutes?"
"I said fine, and I get that too, you know."
"It works for both of us, meaning I'm sure that he feels if the interview's not going good, he wants it to be short."
"And for me, if the interview's not going good, I want it to be short."
"So I'm like, yeah, sure, 30 minutes."
"Is John Kuzek really all that paranoid about his career at this point?"
"I don't know. I mean, I don't know him."
"So then they asked me, is it going to be on television?" And I said it was, and they said, "Where will it air?"
I said, "In demand." They asked, "What's that?"
"It's Howard Stern, man."
"But it's fine."
"It's the Howard TV channel."
"But they weren't being mean or anything. They were more like, they just wanted to know a lot of stuff."
"They're in media, and they're asking what the Howard on demand is?"
"It's the hottest thing in the country."
"I mean, they're asking you, like, you're some local St. Louis station?"
"I don't know. So, I mean, they were nice about it. So then I get the call like 6 o'clock last night, you know, John Kuzek will not be able to make it."
"John Kuzek's a pussy, and that's the bottom line. He makes a commitment to come here."
"But he didn't make the commitment. I'm not going to throw him under the bus that way."
"They didn't make the commitment."
"They never said he's booked."
"Then why do they bug us? Don't bother us."
"It was like, as the whole day went on."
"Well, who is he, fucking Superman? I mean, just fucking either come in or don't. Don't fucking hawk us in China."
"I mean, guys are staying here until 10 o'clock researching the motherfucker."
"I don't know John Kuzek. Did I ever interview him before?"
"No."
"I don't think so."
"Yeah. I mean, I don't know the guy. I'm not all that. Quite frankly, I don't even know fucking shit about the guy. Is he that big a star?"
"I mean, he's one of those guys that, it's a funny thing, we were saying in the office yesterday, he's been in a lot of great movies."
"Right."
"But he's not, like, a huge star. But, you know what I mean? Like, John Kuzek."
"I think we've even turned him down a few times."
"I mean, he can open a romantic comedy, I think is the route."
"I saw his list, Howard. It was fucking awesome."
"His list of what?"
"Chickie bang."
"Yeah, what was he got?"
"I don't know, he went out with Nev Campbell when she was Nev Campbell."
"Of course, he was Earl Campbell."
"I think that they had him linked to, like, Britney Spears at one point."
"When she was Britney Spears."
"He dated them all when they were them."
"Yeah, before they turned into whatever gross thing they had them out."
"Bunch of chicks and stuff. But he's been in some great movies, especially for guys like, you know, movies like 'Eight Men Out,' 'The Shore' thing."
"Say anything."
"Say anything."
"Yeah, I like the guy. I've seen him in movies. I know we've turned him down before, but I figured, 'Well, okay, why don't we check out? See what kind of guy he is.'"
"It's just these publicists. They're so fucking annoying."
"Well, especially when Gary's rearranging the schedule for the person, and they still haven't committed."
"And Gary's calling me every minute, going, 'I want to move to Fallon.' I'm like, 'Wow, the poor guy is getting his fucking balls busted.'"
"And then it's like--"
"And then they don't even show up."
"Don't, tell these publicists, don't approach us until you know your client wants to be on the show for sure."
"It could be a publisher's fault, too."
"Yeah, no, no. It's hard to say--"
"But you know what? If I'm John Cusack, look a little bit into who's managing your fucking life."
"Because you know what happened. They did all this work, and then John Cusack said, 'No, I don't want to do it.'"
"Right, right, right."
"Here we go. John dated Uma Thurman. That's a good one. I'd like to date her. Many driver."
"But again, when she was--"
"Meg Ryan. Ugh. Rebecca Remidgen."
"Where's Rebecca Remidgen?"
"That's right."
"When she was Rebecca Remidgen."
"Rebecca Remain Stamos."
"When did he date her?"
"I don't know."
"Wow, that's amazing."
"Free John, obviously."
"Lily Taylor. I don't know who that is."
"She's--Lily Taylor's the one that got the restraining order against Michael Rappaport."
"She's not that high."
"Allison Eastwood. That's Clint Eastwood's daughter."
"She's cute."
"And Claire Forlani. Didn't she run for governor or something?"
"She's beautiful."
"Who is she?"
"She's an actress. She's just beautiful."
"That's not Lola Forlani."
"No, it's Claire Forlani."
"Claire Forlani and I had the same film agent once at William Morris. It was ten years ago."
"And I walked into their office in Beverly Hills, and she was sitting in the waiting room."
"And she had just done--what the fuck was she in? It was kind of a bomb."
"Meet Joe Black."
"Right. She was the girl."
"Oh, that girl was hot."
"They were talking about going to the premiere of 'Meet Joe Black' like the next night."
"And I had an appointment, and man, she was so stunning in the room."
"I was like looking up, I couldn't stop looking at her."
"Wow. Yeah. Well."
"Hey, so--and then I guess Mark McGrath was due to come in and then--"
"Now that's one--that's another one where we move stuff out of the way to make room for him."
"What's with Mark?"
"Well, they're saying that I ended up talking to his agent at William Morris who books him for commercials."
"And then inevitably I did some phone calls with his manager."
"But they're saying that it was never intended for him to be on the show this trip around."
"That the publicity--"
"But how did they get that so wrong?"
"I know, and they say the--here's the thing that makes you crazy, right? Like with John Cusack."
"The personal PR will blame the movie company. The movie company will blame the personal PR."
"With Mark McGrath."
"I never heard of him."
"Like everybody's everybody, and you can never--you can't get them all in a room and go, 'Who the fuck's telling the truth?'"
"Then just leave us alone."
"So walk to Mark McGrath and see if he wants to come in first before you call us."
"So they were saying he should have never been pinched to us, that it was a huge mistake, that they told him right off the bat that he was going to be busy."
"But he's coming into town to promote--Unilever's a big company, right? Like a proctor gambled it, right?"
"So I guess he did some voiceover for I Can't Believe It's Not Butter."
"And that's what he's promoting."
"Oh, he's promoting--"
"That's a great guess."
"And I think my guess is that the Unilever people don't want him on this show."
"I think at one point there was a discussion that he might have a morals clause in his contract."
"So it freaks people out."
"It's immoral to appear on this show?"
"Evidently, according to Unilever."
"Whatever that is."
"I'm not going to make Mark McGrath ride the Sydney."
"How dumb."
"Let's figure out what's in 'I Can't Believe It's Not Butter.' If it's not butter, what the fuck is it?"
"Yeah, right."
"I mean, what are we putting into our systems?"

"Exactly."
"Yeah, I love 'I Can't Believe It's Not Butter' thinks I'm immoral."
"Well, find out what's in 'I Can't Believe It's Not Butter.' I want to find out."
"But I don't know that's what I--"
"I want to know how good they are."
"But I was telling Howard, like, I take it very personally."
"I take it--like, I love the show."
"You just described what sounds like a nightmarish day."
"And I'm at my son's baseball game last night in the trees going, 'What the fuck is going on?'"
"I don't want anybody to hear me, but I'm like, I'm trying to get to the bottom of it. It's just a big pain."
"Just annoying, but don't worry about it. I mean, you know, it's cool, but--"
"And then, well, I don't know."
"What?"
"And then I told you that other thing that bothered me that I saw this morning."
"I don't know if you want to talk about it, that Joe Rogan responding, the way he doesn't do the show anymore."
"I take all this very personally."
"Why doesn't he do the show anymore?"
"What happened here?"
"I read that, too."
"What did he say?"
"Here's what he said."
"Matt, I put you on the air, but if you don't mind waiting a second."
"Yeah, no problem."
"So I read this explanation by Joe, evidently, I guess, you know, we had gone to like a strip club, and we had girls on the show, you know, like stripper types on the show."
"Yeah."
"And we knew Joe pretty well, you know, I like Joe a lot."
"Absolutely."
"I think Joe's a real talented guy."
"When I started doing the show, he was in the regular rotation in the Jackie chair, and you know, he was a big guest."

"Yeah, and Joe said, you know, Joe says many complimentary things. He says, you know, I've always considered the Howard Stern show, the Johnny Carson show of my generation, that that's the show you do."

"He said, but what happened was, I got on the air, and you know, it's funny, I was just kind of saying this to kind of create something to talk about and make Joe, you know, respond to this, but I noticed that Joe, whenever there's like a hot stripper chick, he'd start like cursing him out a little."

"It's funny, like I didn't mean it like a whole heavy discussion, but I said, Joe doesn't appear to really like women, like, you know, he'd get this hostility."

"Yeah, like he'd always get the fight. I thought it was funny, like, which could be his thing, you know, like, it'd even go, no, no, and then he'd come on the show and go, no, no, no, I like women, you know, because, you know, they're just whores of this one and that one."
"He was always calling everyone a whore."

"And I'd say to him, why are they a whore? Just because they're a stripper. I mean, it doesn't mean they're a whore, you know, and it was funny. I thought it was funny, interesting, compelling conversation and gave him sort of an edge and a hook."
"Yeah, I was here for a lot of those conversations, I remember, and he never seemed irritated."

"No, and he took offense, like he said, you know, Howard says I hate women, and I don't hate women, and if I had a friend who threw me under the bus like that, you know, I just wouldn't talk to them anymore, and I love Howard, and I love what he does, but quite frankly, no friend of mine would say that I hate women, and I guess she took it, like, literally, like, I, like, I, like, I was..."

"And so it's festering and festering and I'm calling out of good will and I want Joe to come join us in Sirius, I want Joe to come, you know, do some stuff and I, you know, I'm getting stonewalled and I don't know that there's some comedian out there that's got this festering thing going on, almost like a hatred."

"And what's weird to me is, I mean, I don't recall Joe being a, the kind of guest that was on every talk show, I mean, we, we kind of always put him on and always promoted his career and loved his routines on CD."
"Oh, absolutely."
"Especially, he's good for the show and the show's really good for him."
"But we had Joe on, especially in that odd period for him, After News Radio, but before Fear Factor, where he probably wasn't getting as many calls to be on shows, you know, as we were giving him."
"And again, I, since this has been going on, I've seen him at a couple of events this past week and I saw him and he could not be friendlier, nicer, say hi, how you doing?"
"He wasn't disrespectful, like he kind of said, you know, I really do love Howard, I love the show and everything, but, but, you know, it's just like I thought I got kind of, gassed, you know."
"It sounds like something that, if, if you would have him come in here and he would want to come in, it could be patched up and like, but he's not willing to come in, that's the point."
"That's the point, he's not willing to come in, that's so ridiculous."
"He's like one of those guys who needs a grudge, you know?"
"I agree, there's a lot of Pat Cooper in here."
"Yeah."
"You got to pick and choose your battles and Joe is a man, this is not the one to pick, it could be settled in two seconds."
"He's in a battle with himself."
"Yeah, I don't, I don't feel any battle, I don't hate the guy, and I don't feel punished in any way, you know, I mean, if Joe wants to come in, fine, if he doesn't, I'll continue to carry on."
"And I get pumped because I really liked the guy, I thought he was good on the show and I liked him all the other time."
"And then you don't seem to be great for serious, too."
"The other thing that's weird about Howard is after that comment that he said that you made, I think he did our show a bunch of times, I think it took time."
"For him to sink in."
"Yeah."

"Yeah."
"Oh, happy anniversary."
"Thank you."

"Well, I mean, you'd have to know the game and I'm assuming if you're sitting there."

"It's about pawn structure and how you keep your pawns in a certain, a pawn island is a lot more beneficial in the game."
"So keep them together rather than spreading them out?"
"That's right, and it's the way they're linked."
"Okay."
"You can create it, you know."
"Question two, what is a battery and which pieces can you use to form a battery in chess?"
"A rook."
"Mm-hmm."
"A rook."
"That's not a completely correct answer."
"A queen?"
"No, it's either a bishop or a rook."
"I do it, what is this?"
"He wants to know how well you're doing in chess."
"I guess I don't, honestly, I don't know what a battery is."
"Okay, it's when you line up your rooks, like you have them on the same..."
"Oh, that's what I said, a rook, yeah, and you, like a battering ram, you go up and down."
"Well, no, it's where you line two of the same pieces up right beside each other, like you have a bishop."
"Right, okay, I know what that is."
"Or you have a rook and then another rook right behind an inch."
"I have a question for you, what's a good phone caller?"

"Joe is very much into, like, hand-to-hand fighting and martial arts."
"He's manly."
"Bear-knuckle thing."
"He could definitely kick some eye-ass, I know that, he's like a karate man."
"Okay, that's pretty much it for the chess question, man."
"I guess I didn't do so well."
"All right, thanks, brother."
"All right, that's Matt with the chess quiz."
"It sounds like you've never played before."
"Yeah, it doesn't sound like I'm doing too well."
"I sound pretty bad, I'm pretty sure."
"After a year of study, I'm shocked."
"Yeah, I don't know."
"Pawn Island."
"That was, I mean, I'm sure Matt was a nice guy, but that was sort of a dull call."

"Yeah, we were having a good show."
